decisions.title: Ship Sift Edge metadata completeness (units, descriptions, enums) in Q2
decisions.context: ENG-10356, ENG-10688 show GUI and sync work in flight. Core product capability—metadata richness is table stakes for 'Embed Deeper' and decision quality. Affects canvas, explore, python rules downstream.

arguments.for: Metadata is foundational signal evidence. Unlocks better canvas annotations, python rule context, and explore search. Customers expect complete data dictionaries. Low-hanging fruit for North Star (decisions require rich context).
arguments.against: May delay other Edge features. Sync complexity could introduce bugs. Customers haven't explicitly complained—may not move North Star needle immediately.

decisions.title: Prioritize Canvas Archive + Version History (ENG-9442, ENG-9443) over Make Copy (ENG-9574)
decisions.context: Canvas is core to 'Signal>Evidence>Decision' loop. Archive/version = decision audit trail (moat). Make Copy = convenience. Version history enables compliance, reproducibility, trust—critical for defense/aerospace customers.

arguments.for: Version history builds moat (IP, compliance). Archive reduces clutter, increases canvas quality. Both support 'decisions captured' by making old decisions traceable. Defense customers need audit trails.
arguments.against: Make Copy is faster to ship, user-requested. Version history adds DB/storage complexity. May slow other canvas features (markdown, metadata).

decisions.title: Accelerate Python Rules metadata + remote files (ENG-7375, ENG-8757) to enable 'Ask SIFT' context
decisions.context: Python Rules are decision automation layer. Metadata + remote files = richer rule context for LLM (Ask SIFT). Remote files unblock customer-specific models, external data. Critical for 'Build the Moat'—rules as competitive differentiator.
decisions.blockedBy: Ask SIFT context ingestion architecture not finalized; ENG-10727 (gov queue testing) may surface security constraints for remote files.

arguments.for: Rules with metadata + external data = smarter decisions. Ask SIFT can explain 'why' a rule fired (moat). Remote files unlock ML models, third-party APIs—huge customer ask. Metadata supports 'Nail the Core' (reliable rule execution).
arguments.against: Remote files increase attack surface, require security review. Metadata adds dev overhead. Ask SIFT integration timeline unclear—may build capability before it's usable.

decisions.title: Invest in scatterplot + geo map family support (ENG-6412, DES-116) for Explore V2
decisions.context: Families (grouping channels) are core Explore V2 pattern. Scatterplot/geo maps are high-value viz for aerospace (trajectories, telemetry). Embedding deeper = supporting complex analysis workflows.

arguments.for: Scatterplot families unlock correlation analysis (e.g., thrust vs. altitude). Geo maps critical for flight test, satellite ops. Both increase 'decisions captured' by enabling spatial/relational insights. Differentiated capability.
arguments.against: Complex UI/UX (DES-116 implies design challenges). May distract from core Explore V2 stability. Customers can use external tools (e.g., Python notebooks) for now.

decisions.title: Formalize ops/CLAUDE.md practice (ENG-10718) as standard across all teams
decisions.context: ENG-10718 is single ticket but represents knowledge management pattern. CLAUDE.md = context for AI-assisted dev, onboarding, decision archaeology. Scales 'Signal>Evidence>Decision' internally.

arguments.for: Institutionalizes decision context. Accelerates onboarding, debugging, AI tooling. Low cost, high leverage. Models 'decisions captured' culture internally. Supports 'Nail the Core' (reduce tribal knowledge risk).
arguments.against: Adds process overhead. Developers may see as busywork. Requires enforcement/tooling to prevent doc rot. Unclear ROI measurement.

decisions.title: Coordinate Heart Aerospace + Starpath announcements (CAGR-54, CAGR-37) to demonstrate 'Embed Deeper' momentum
decisions.context: Heart Aerospace (electric aviation) + Starpath (space logistics) are marquee customers. Co-branded stories + MSA = proof points for 'embed deeper' pillar. Marketing timing matters for industry visibility.

arguments.for: Dual announcements create 'Sift is everywhere in aerospace' narrative. Starpath MSA = revenue signal. Heart video/assets = emotional storytelling. Supports sales pipeline (proof of embed deeper). Modern Day Marine + DMV Tech Week (CAGR-22) timing = amplification.
arguments.against: Customer approval delays common. Starpath MSA may not close in time. Splitting focus across two stories dilutes impact. Risk of one announcement overshadowing the other.

decisions.title: Allocate eng resources to FedRAMP merge (ENG-10729) vs. new feature work
decisions.context: FedRAMP (Redpanda merge) is 'Build the Moat'—unlocks government contracts. Competes with canvas, edge, python rules features. Timing affects Q2 revenue targets.

arguments.for: FedRAMP unblocks multi-million dollar contracts. Moat-building (competitors can't easily replicate). Redpanda merge is technical debt—delays compound. Gov customers are patient but expect timelines.
arguments.against: Diverts eng from North Star metrics (decisions captured). FedRAMP sales cycle is long—immediate impact unclear. Existing commercial customers may churn if features slow. Merge complexity may introduce instability.

keyResults.name: Decision audit features shipped (version history, archive)

timeAudit.weekSummary: Heavy coordination week—CAGR marketing tickets (CNN, Modern Day Marine, Austin trip) imply cross-functional syncs. FedRAMP merge + canvas features suggest eng alignment sessions. 8 free hours concentrated Tue/Thu.
alerts: Only 2 focus blocks today—canvas metadata (ENG-10094), python rules decisions at risk of shallow review
22 meetings this week = 55% of work time. Consider batching CAGR marketing reviews (7 tickets = potential for 7 separate syncs)
3 one-on-ones today—may crowd out decision-making bandwidth. Recommend async updates for non-urgent topics.
